Take a look at commit e45daa39e8ae5bcdc37458e6e5d20df68764bb47 and understand what we did there to address poor performance for a large number of Claude Code ssesions.

This perf. issue was fixed for `reindex`. We still see this super slow O(n^2) type slowness for `specstory sync`. Do we have the exact same issue there? If so, why did the fix not fix it in both places... does that point to some duplication that shouldn't be there? If not the exact same issue, do we have a similar issue that needs a similar fix?

If you need a large CC project to check the `sync` performance with, the project for the cwd is large and very slow on `specstory sync`

`git show e45daa39e8ae5bcdc37458e6e5d20df68764bb47 --stat`

```text
commit e45daa39e8ae5bcdc37458e6e5d20df68764bb47
Author: Greg Ceccarelli <gregce@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 26 13:56:19 2026 -0400

    Make `specstory reindex` O(N) instead of O(N²)
    
    Reindexing ~28k sessions (25k of them Codex) took hours and never finished —
    throughput decelerated as it ran, the signature of an O(N²) algorithm. Two
    compounding quadratics were responsible:
    
    1. Codex re-walked the entire ~/.codex/sessions tree per session. buildSession
       called GetAgentChatSession(cwd, sessionID), which walks the whole
       year/month/day tree opening and first-line-parsing every file until the match
       is found — even though enumeration already recorded the exact file in
       ref.NativePath. At 25k sessions that is ~N²/2 file opens.
    
    2. The standalone FTS5 delete scanned the whole index per row. Every write did
       DELETE FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent=? AND session_id=?, but both columns are
       UNINDEXED, so FTS5 scans all rows on each delete — O(N²) over a full build, on
       the single writer goroutine. This hit every provider, not just Codex, and is
       why even Claude was slow.
    
    Fixes:
    
    - Add spi.PathSessionReader, an optional provider capability to parse a session
      directly from its known native path. Codex implements it
      (GetAgentChatSessionByPath), so reindex parses ref.NativePath in O(1) instead
      of re-discovering it. This is also more correct: the row's freshness
      fingerprint is stat'd from NativePath, so the body and the fingerprint now come
      from the same file (Codex's by-id search could otherwise pick a different one).
    
    - Skip the FTS delete for brand-new sessions. selectWork marks an item isNew when
      it is absent from the fingerprint set (and not under --force, where the set is
      intentionally empty); upsertTx then skips the delete, since a new session has no
      FTS row to remove. Re-index of an existing session keeps the delete, so replaces
      stay correct.
    
    - Bump reindexVersion 4 -> 5 so existing rows re-parse once under the corrected
      pipeline.
    
    Result: a full from-scratch rebuild of 28,417 sessions in ~5 min (previously
    hours, unfinished). Verified: sessions and FTS row counts match exactly (28,417
    each) with zero duplicate FTS rows from the skipped deletes.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 specstory-cli/pkg/cmd/reindex.go                 | 39 +++++++++++++++++++-----
 specstory-cli/pkg/providers/codexcli/provider.go | 19 ++++++++++++
 specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex/store.go          | 19 ++++++++++--
 specstory-cli/pkg/spi/global.go                  | 18 +++++++++++
 4 files changed, 84 insertions(+), 11 deletions(-)
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
commit e45daa39e8ae5bcdc37458e6e5d20df68764bb47
Author: Greg Ceccarelli <gregce@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 26 13:56:19 2026 -0400

    Make `specstory reindex` O(N) instead of O(N²)
    
    Reindexing ~28k sessions (25k of them Codex) took hours and never finished —
    throughput decelerated as it ran, the signature of an O(N²) algorithm. Two
    compounding quadratics were responsible:
    
    1. Codex re-walked the entire ~/.codex/sessions tree per session. buildSession
       called GetAgentChatSession(cwd, sessionID), which walks the whole
       year/month/day tree opening and first-line-parsing every file until the match
       is found — even though enumeration already recorded the exact file in
       ref.NativePath. At 25k sessions that is ~N²/2 file opens.
    
    2. The standalone FTS5 delete scanned the whole index per row. Every write did
       DELETE FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent=? AND session_id=?, but both columns are
       UNINDEXED, so FTS5 scans all rows on each delete — O(N²) over a full build, on
       the single writer goroutine. This hit every provider, not just Codex, and is
       why even Claude was slow.
    
    Fixes:
    
    - Add spi.PathSessionReader, an optional provider capability to parse a session
      directly from its known native path. Codex implements it
      (GetAgentChatSessionByPath), so reindex parses ref.NativePath in O(1) instead
      of re-discovering it. This is also more correct: the row's freshness
      fingerprint is stat'd from NativePath, so the body and the fingerprint now come
      from the same file (Codex's by-id search could otherwise pick a different one).
    
    - Skip the FTS delete for brand-new sessions. selectWork marks an item isNew when
      it is absent from the fingerprint set (and not under --force, where the set is
      intentionally empty); upsertTx then skips the delete, since a new session has no
      FTS row to remove. Re-index of an existing session keeps the delete, so replaces
      stay correct.
    
    - Bump reindexVersion 4 -> 5 so existing rows re-parse once under the corrected
      pipeline.
    
    Result: a full from-scratch rebuild of 28,417 sessions in ~5 min (previously
    hours, unfinished). Verified: sessions and FTS row counts match exactly (28,417
    each) with zero duplicate FTS rows from the skipped deletes.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -507,6 +516,20 @@ func buildSession(item reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string) ses
 	return sess

+// parseFullSession reads a session's full data for indexing. It prefers a provider's
+// path-keyed parse (spi.PathSessionReader) using the ref's already-known NativePath, which
+// avoids the provider's by-id discovery search — for Codex that search walks the entire
+// ~/.codex/sessions tree, so this is the difference between an O(N) and an O(N²) reindex.
+// Providers without the capability (or refs lacking a NativePath) fall back to the by-id
+// lookup. Parsing the known NativePath is also more consistent: the row's freshness
+// fingerprint is stat'd from NativePath, so the body now comes from that same file.
+func parseFullSession(prov spi.Provider, ref spi.GlobalSessionRef) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
+	if pr, ok := prov.(spi.PathSessionReader); ok && ref.NativePath != "" {
+		return pr.GetAgentChatSessionByPath(ref.NativePath, ref.OriginCwd, false)
+	}
+	return prov.GetAgentChatSession(ref.OriginCwd, ref.SessionID, false)
+}
+
 // drainToStore writes parsed sessions to the index in batched transactions.
 func drainToStore(store *sessionindex.Store, ch <-chan sessionindex.Session) error {
 	batch := make([]sessionindex.Session, 0, reindexWriteBatch)
diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/codexcli/provider.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/codexcli/provider.go
index 4460aca..ff8e7b9 100644
--- a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/codexcli/provider.go
+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/codexcli/provider.go
@@ -330,6 +330,25 @@ func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSession(projectPath string, sessionID string, deb
 	return processSessionToAgentChat(&sessions[0], projectPath, debugRaw)
 }
 
+// GetAgentChatSessionByPath parses a single Codex session directly from its native file
+// path, implementing spi.PathSessionReader. It skips findCodexSessions' whole-tree walk —
+// which is what makes the by-id GetAgentChatSession O(N) per call, and a reindex of every
+// session O(N²) — by reading session_meta from the known file and handing off to the same
+// processSessionToAgentChat the by-id path uses. originCwd is the workspace root, matching
+// what GetAgentChatSession passes as projectPath.
+func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSessionByPath(nativePath string, originCwd string, debugRaw bool) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
+	meta, err := loadCodexSessionMeta(nativePath)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to load codex session meta: %w", err)
+	}
+	info := codexSessionInfo{
+		SessionID:   strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID),
+		SessionPath: nativePath,
+		Meta:        meta,
+	}
+	return processSessionToAgentChat(&info, originCwd, debugRaw)
+}
+
 // ExecAgentAndWatch executes the Codex CLI in interactive mode and monitors for session updates.
 // The function blocks until the Codex CLI exits. During execution, it watches for JSONL file changes
 // and invokes sessionCallback for each update, enabling real-time markdown generation and cloud sync.

index 3c13a46..d89c3d2 100644
--- a/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex/store.go
+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex/store.go
@@ -50,6 +50,13 @@ type Session struct {
 	// Body is the full conversation text, indexed into sessions_fts (not persisted on
 	// the sessions row). Left empty on rows read back out of the index.
 	Body string
+
+	// IsNew is a write-time hint, not a persisted column: when true, the caller has
+	// determined this (agent, session_id) has no existing index row, so upsertTx can skip
+	// the delete-before-insert on the standalone FTS table (which otherwise scans the whole
+	// FTS, since session_id/agent are UNINDEXED). Leave false when unsure — the delete is
+	// then kept, which is always correct.
+	IsNew bool
 }
 
 // Fingerprint identifies an indexed session's freshness: the native file's size and
@@ -213,9 +220,15 @@ func upsertTx(tx *sql.Tx, sess Session) error {
 		sess.Size, sess.Mtime, sess.IndexVersion, sess.IndexedAt); err != nil {
 		return fmt.Errorf("upsert session row: %w", err)
 	}
-	if _, err := tx.Exec(`DELETE FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`,
-		sess.Agent, sess.SessionID); err != nil {
-		return fmt.Errorf("clear fts row: %w", err)
+	// Replace the old FTS row. Skipped for brand-new sessions (sess.IsNew): the delete can't
+	// use an index (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED) so it scans the whole FTS — at 25k+
+	// sessions a from-scratch build turns that into O(N²). A new session has no row to
+	// delete, so the scan would be pure waste.
+	if !sess.IsNew {
+		if _, err := tx.Exec(`DELETE FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`,
+			sess.Agent, sess.SessionID); err != nil {
+			return fmt.Errorf("clear fts row: %w", err)
+		}
 	}
 	if _, err := tx.Exec(`INSERT INTO sessions_fts (session_id, agent, name, body) VALUES (?,?,?,?)`,
 		sess.SessionID, sess.Agent, sess.Name, sess.Body); err != nil {
diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/global.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/global.go
index f1841a4..ca8a84f 100644
--- a/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/global.go
+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/global.go
@@ -19,3 +19,21 @@ type GlobalSessionRef struct {
 	NativePath string // absolute path the provider opens to read this session
 	OriginCwd  string // working directory the session was launched from (-> project_id)
 }
+
+// PathSessionReader is an OPTIONAL capability a Provider may implement: parse a single
+// session directly from its already-known native file path, skipping the by-id discovery
+// search that GetAgentChatSession performs.
+//
+// Why: some providers locate a session by scanning their native store. Codex's by-id
+// lookup walks the entire ~/.codex/sessions tree on every call, so resolving N sessions
+// that way is O(N²). reindex already holds each session's exact file path
+// (GlobalSessionRef.NativePath) from enumeration, so a path-keyed parse turns that back
+// into O(N). reindex prefers this when a provider implements it and the ref carries a
+// NativePath, and falls back to GetAgentChatSession otherwise.
+//
+// originCwd is the session's originating working directory (GlobalSessionRef.OriginCwd),
+// passed through as the workspace root for path normalization — matching what
+// GetAgentChatSession receives as projectPath.
+type PathSessionReader interface {
+	GetAgentChatSessionByPath(nativePath string, originCwd string, debugRaw bool) (*AgentChatSession, error)
+}
```

```text
300	func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, progress spi.ProgressCallback) ([]spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
301		// Get the Claude Code project directory
302		claudeProjectDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(projectPath)
303		if err != nil {
304			return nil, err
305		}
306	
307		// Check if project directory exists
308		if _, err := os.Stat(claudeProjectDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
309			return []spi.AgentChatSession{}, nil // No sessions if no project
310		}
311	
312		// Parse JSONL files with progress callback
313		parser := NewJSONLParser()
314		err = parser.ParseProjectSessionsWithProgress(claudeProjectDir, progress)
315		if err != nil {
316			return nil, err
317		}
318	
319		// Convert sessions to AgentChatSession structs
320		result := make([]spi.AgentChatSession, 0, len(parser.Sessions))
321		for _, session := range parser.Sessions {
322			// Skip empty sessions (no records)
323			if len(session.Records) == 0 {
324				continue
325			}
326	
327			// Process session (filter warmup, generate SessionData)
328			chatSession := processSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
329			if chatSession != nil {
330				result = append(result, *chatSession)
331			}
332		}
333	
334		return result, nil
335	}
```

```text
393	// LiveIndexer keeps ~/.specstory/sessions.db current in real time, alongside the markdown
394	// files that watch/run/resume already write as a session changes. It holds one writer handle
395	// for the command's lifetime and upserts a single row per detected update, reusing the same
396	// content derivation as reindex. The project is resolved once (a watch/run has a fixed cwd).
397	//
398	// Everything is best-effort: real-time indexing must never disrupt the agent or the markdown
399	// write, so construction failures yield a nil *LiveIndexer (whose methods are no-ops) and
400	// per-update errors are logged at debug. A live row carries no native-file fingerprint
401	// (Size/Mtime/NativePath are unknown without enumerating), so the next reindex re-verifies and
402	// rewrites it with a stable fingerprint — the live row is correct and queryable in the interim.
403	type LiveIndexer struct {
404		store       *sessionindex.Store
405		cwd         string
406		projectID   string
407		projectName string
408		indexedAt   string
409	
410		mu       sync.Mutex
411		lastSeen map[string]string // session id -> last indexed UpdatedAt, to skip no-op upserts
412	}
413	
414	// NewLiveIndexer opens the index for writing and resolves the project for cwd. It returns nil
415	// (not an error) when the index can't be opened, so the caller keeps running with real-time
416	// indexing simply disabled. The caller owns Close.
417	func NewLiveIndexer(cwd string) *LiveIndexer {
418		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
419		if err != nil {
420			slog.Debug("live index: no db path; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
421			return nil
422		}
423		store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
424		if err != nil {
425			slog.Debug("live index: cannot open index; real-time indexing disabled", "error", err)
426			return nil
427		}
428		projectID, projectName, err := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
429		if err != nil || projectID == "" {
430			projectID, projectName = unknownProjectID, ""
431		}
432		return &LiveIndexer{
433			store:       store,
434			cwd:         cwd,
435			projectID:   projectID,
436			projectName: projectName,
437			indexedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
438			lastSeen:    map[string]string{},
439		}
440	}
441	
442	// Record upserts the live session into the index. agentID is the provider's registry id (e.g.
443	// "claude"). It skips the write when the session's last activity hasn't advanced since the
444	// previous record (the watcher can fire repeatedly without new content). Safe on a nil
445	// receiver, and serialized so concurrent provider watchers don't contend on the single writer.
446	func (li *LiveIndexer) Record(agentID string, sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
447		if li == nil || sess == nil || sess.SessionData == nil {
448			return
449		}
450		data := sess.SessionData
451	
452		updatedAt := sess.CreatedAt
453		if ts := lastTimestamp(data); ts != "" {
454			updatedAt = ts
455		}
456	
457		li.mu.Lock()
458		defer li.mu.Unlock()
459	
460		// Nothing advanced since the last write → skip the (FTS-heavy) upsert.
461		if updatedAt != "" && li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] == updatedAt {
462			return
463		}
464	
465		userTurns, totalTurns := countTurns(data)
466		row := sessionindex.Session{
467			ProjectID:    li.projectID,
468			ProjectName:  li.projectName,
469			Agent:        agentID,
470			SessionID:    sess.SessionID,
471			CreatedAt:    sess.CreatedAt,
472			UpdatedAt:    updatedAt,
473			UserTurns:    userTurns,
474			TotalTurns:   totalTurns,
475			Slug:         sess.Slug,
476			OriginCwd:    li.cwd,
477			IndexVersion: reindexVersion,
478			IndexedAt:    li.indexedAt,
479			Body:         flattenBody(data),
480		}
481		if err := li.store.Upsert(row); err != nil {
482			slog.Debug("live index: upsert failed", "sessionId", sess.SessionID, "error", err)
483			return
484		}
485		li.lastSeen[sess.SessionID] = updatedAt
486	}
487	
488	// Close releases the writer handle. Safe on a nil receiver.
489	func (li *LiveIndexer) Close() {
490		if li == nil {
491			return
492		}
493		if err := li.store.Close(); err != nil {
494			slog.Debug("live index: close failed", "error", err)
495		}
496	}
497	
498	// buildSession turns one enumeration ref into a fully-built index row. Every session
499	// yields a row from its metadata; the body + turn counts + last-activity time are
500	// ENRICHED via a full parse only when the originating cwd lets us re-locate the
501	// session (so Cursor, whose cwd is unknown for now, lands metadata-only). Nothing is
502	// dropped.
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
200			if err := rows.Scan(&agent, &sessionID, &fp.Size, &fp.Mtime, &fp.Version); err != nil {
201				return nil, err
202			}
203			out[FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID)] = fp
204		}
205		return out, rows.Err()
206	}
207	
208	// FingerprintKey is the map key for a session's fingerprint: agent + NUL + session_id.
209	func FingerprintKey(agent, sessionID string) string {
210		return agent + "\x00" + sessionID
211	}
212	
213	// sessionUpsertStmts are the per-row statements UpsertBatch prepares once and reuses across
214	// every row in a transaction, so the SQL is parsed once per batch instead of once per row.
215	type sessionUpsertStmts struct {
216		insSession *sql.Stmt
217		delFTS     *sql.Stmt
218		insFTS     *sql.Stmt
219	}
220	
221	// upsertOne writes one session's row and full-text row using the batch's prepared statements.
222	// FTS5 standalone tables are not auto-synced, so the FTS row is replaced by hand — except for
223	// brand-new sessions (sess.IsNew), where the delete is skipped: it can't use an index
224	// (session_id/agent are UNINDEXED) so it scans the whole FTS, and a new session has no row to
225	// delete anyway. At 25k+ sessions that skip is what keeps a from-scratch build out of O(N²).
226	func upsertOne(st sessionUpsertStmts, sess Session) error {
227		if _, err := st.insSession.Exec(
228			sess.ProjectID, sess.ProjectName, sess.Agent, sess.SessionID, sess.CreatedAt, sess.UpdatedAt,
229			sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns, sess.Slug, sess.Name, sess.NativePath, sess.OriginCwd,
230			sess.Size, sess.Mtime, sess.IndexVersion, sess.IndexedAt); err != nil {
231			return fmt.Errorf("upsert session row: %w", err)
232		}
233		if !sess.IsNew {
234			if _, err := st.delFTS.Exec(sess.Agent, sess.SessionID); err != nil {
235				return fmt.Errorf("clear fts row: %w", err)
236			}
237		}
238		if _, err := st.insFTS.Exec(sess.SessionID, sess.Agent, sess.Name, sess.Body); err != nil {
239			return fmt.Errorf("insert fts row: %w", err)
240		}
241		return nil
242	}
243	
244	// prepareUpsert prepares the three per-row statements on tx and returns them with a closer.
245	func prepareUpsert(tx *sql.Tx) (sessionUpsertStmts, func(), error) {
246		insSession, err := tx.Prepare(`INSERT OR REPLACE INTO sessions (` + sessionColumns + `)
247			VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)`)
248		if err != nil {
249			return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, fmt.Errorf("prepare session upsert: %w", err)
250		}
251		delFTS, err := tx.Prepare(`DELETE FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`)
252		if err != nil {
253			_ = insSession.Close()
254			return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, fmt.Errorf("prepare fts delete: %w", err)
255		}
256		insFTS, err := tx.Prepare(`INSERT INTO sessions_fts (session_id, agent, name, body) VALUES (?,?,?,?)`)
257		if err != nil {
258			_ = insSession.Close()
259			_ = delFTS.Close()
260			return sessionUpsertStmts{}, func() {}, fmt.Errorf("prepare fts insert: %w", err)
261		}
262		closer := func() {
263			_ = insSession.Close()
264			_ = delFTS.Close()
265			_ = insFTS.Close()
266		}
267		return sessionUpsertStmts{insSession: insSession, delFTS: delFTS, insFTS: insFTS}, closer, nil
268	}
269	
270	// Upsert inserts or replaces a single session row and its full-text row, atomically. A
271	// re-index of the same session (same agent + session_id) replaces both in lockstep.
272	func (s *Store) Upsert(sess Session) error {
273		return s.UpsertBatch([]Session{sess})
274	}
275	
276	// UpsertBatch writes many sessions in one transaction — the write path for `reindex`,
277	// which batches to avoid thousands of tiny WAL commits.
278	func (s *Store) UpsertBatch(sessions []Session) error {
279		if len(sessions) == 0 {
280			return nil
281		}
282		tx, err := s.db.Begin()
283		if err != nil {
284			return fmt.Errorf("begin upsert: %w", err)
285		}
286		committed := false
287		defer func() {
288			if !committed {
289				_ = tx.Rollback()
290			}
291		}()
292	
293		st, closeStmts, err := prepareUpsert(tx)
294		if err != nil {
295			return err
296		}
297		defer closeStmts()
298	
299		for _, sess := range sessions {
300			if err := upsertOne(st, sess); err != nil {
301				return err
302			}
303		}
304	
305		if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
306			return fmt.Errorf("commit upsert: %w", err)
307		}
308		committed = true
309		return nil
310	}
311	
312	// Count returns the number of indexed sessions.
313	func (s *Store) Count() (int, error) {
314		var n int
315		if err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions`).Scan(&n); err != nil {
316			return 0, err
317		}
318		return n, nil
319	}
320	
321	// ProjectCount returns the number of distinct attributed projects in the index
322	// (excluding the unknownID bucket).
323	func (s *Store) ProjectCount(unknownID string) (int, error) {
324		var n int
325		err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT project_id) FROM sessions WHERE project_id != ?`, unknownID).Scan(&n)
326		return n, err
327	}
328	
329	// UnattributedCount returns the number of sessions in the unknownID bucket.
```

```text
83	func (p *JSONLParser) ParseProjectSessionsWithProgress(projectPath string, progress spi.ProgressCallback) error {
84		return p.parseProjectSessionsInternal(projectPath, "", progress)
85	}
86	
87	// ParseProjectSessionsForSession parses JSONL files that match a specific session ID.
88	// Files that don't contain the given sessionID are skipped for performance.
89	// If sessionID is empty, this behaves identically to ParseProjectSessions.
90	func (p *JSONLParser) ParseProjectSessionsForSession(projectPath string, silent bool, sessionID string) error {
91		return p.parseProjectSessionsInternal(projectPath, sessionID, nil)
92	}
93	
94	// parseProjectSessionsInternal is the shared implementation for all parsing methods.
95	// progress: optional callback for reporting progress (nil = no progress reporting)
96	func (p *JSONLParser) parseProjectSessionsInternal(projectPath string, targetSessionID string, progress spi.ProgressCallback) error {
97		// Track overall parsing time
98		parseStartTime := time.Now()
99		slog.Info("ParseProjectSessions: Starting JSONL parsing")
100	
101		// Step 1: Collect JSONL file paths (first pass for accurate progress count)
102		var jsonlFiles []string
103		err := filepath.Walk(projectPath, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
104			if err != nil {
105				return err
106			}
107			if info.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(path, ".jsonl") {
108				return nil
109			}
110			if targetSessionID != "" {
111				foundSessionID, matchErr := extractSessionIDFromFile(path)
112				if matchErr != nil {
113					slog.Warn("ParseProjectSessions: Failed to check session ID, parsing anyway",
114						"file", path,
115						"error", matchErr)
116				} else if foundSessionID != targetSessionID {
117					return nil
118				}
119			}
120			jsonlFiles = append(jsonlFiles, path)
121			return nil
122		})
123		if err != nil {
124			return fmt.Errorf("failed to scan project directory %s: %w", projectPath, err)
125		}
126	
127		totalFiles := len(jsonlFiles)
128		slog.Info("ParseProjectSessions: Found JSONL files", "count", totalFiles)
129	
130		// Step 2: Parse files with progress reporting
131		scanStartTime := time.Now()
132		allRecords := []JSONLRecord{}
133		for i, path := range jsonlFiles {
134			records, err := p.parseSessionFile(path)
135			if err != nil {
136				return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse file %s: %w", path, err)
137			}
138			allRecords = append(allRecords, records...)
139	
140			// Report progress after each file
141			if progress != nil {
142				progress(i+1, totalFiles)
143			}
144		}
145	
146		scanDuration := time.Since(scanStartTime)
147		slog.Info("ParseProjectSessions: File scanning completed", "duration", formatDuration(scanDuration), "records", len(allRecords))
148	
149		// Step 3: Eliminate duplicates by uuid (keep earliest by timestamp)
150		dedupStartTime := time.Now()
151		slog.Debug("ParseProjectSessions: Eliminating duplicates", "records", len(allRecords))
152		uniqueRecords := p.eliminateDuplicates(allRecords)
153		slog.Debug("ParseProjectSessions: After deduplication", "uniqueRecords", len(uniqueRecords))
154		dedupDuration := time.Since(dedupStartTime)
155		slog.Debug("ParseProjectSessions: Deduplication completed", "duration", formatDuration(dedupDuration))
156	
157		// Store all records for searching
158		p.Records = uniqueRecords
159	
160		// Step 4: Build parent/child DAGs
161		slog.Debug("ParseProjectSessions: Building DAGs")
162		dagStartTime := time.Now()
163		dags := p.buildDAGs(uniqueRecords)
164		dagDuration := time.Since(dagStartTime)
165		slog.Debug("ParseProjectSessions: DAG building completed", "duration", formatDuration(dagDuration), "dagCount", len(dags))
166	
167		// Step 5: Merge DAGs with the same session ID (handles resumed sessions)
168		mergeStartTime := time.Now()
169		mergedDags := p.mergeDagsWithSameSessionId(dags)
170		mergeDuration := time.Since(mergeStartTime)
171		slog.Debug("ParseProjectSessions: DAG merging completed", "duration", formatDuration(mergeDuration),
172			"beforeCount", len(dags), "afterCount", len(mergedDags))
173	
174		// Step 6: Flatten each DAG into an array ordered by timestamp
175		flattenStartTime := time.Now()
176		sessions := []Session{}
177		for _, dag := range mergedDags {
178			flattenedDAG := p.flattenDAG(dag)
179			if len(flattenedDAG) == 0 {
180				continue
181			}
182	
183			// Step 7: The session is the sessionId of the head
184			sessionId := ""
185			if sid, ok := flattenedDAG[0].Data["sessionId"].(string); ok {
186				sessionId = sid
187			} else {
188				// If head doesn't have sessionId, find first one in the array
189				for _, record := range flattenedDAG {
190					if sid, ok := record.Data["sessionId"].(string); ok {
191						sessionId = sid
192						break
193					}
194				}
195			}
196	
197			// Skip if no valid sessionId found
198			if sessionId == "" {
199				slog.Debug("Skipping DAG with no valid sessionId")
200				continue
201			}
202	
203			// Log session creation details
204			if len(flattenedDAG) > 0 {
205				rootTimestamp := ""
206				if ts, ok := flattenedDAG[0].Data["timestamp"].(string); ok {
207					rootTimestamp = ts
208				}
209				slog.Debug("Creating session",
210					"sessionId", sessionId,
211					"rootTimestamp", rootTimestamp,
212					"recordCount", len(flattenedDAG))
213			}
214	
215			sessions = append(sessions, Session{
216				SessionUuid: sessionId,
217				Records:     flattenedDAG,
218			})
219		}
220	
221		p.Sessions = sessions
222		flattenDuration := time.Since(flattenStartTime)
223		slog.Debug("ParseProjectSessions: Flattening completed", "duration", formatDuration(flattenDuration))
224	
225		// Log total parsing time
226		totalDuration := time.Since(parseStartTime)
227		slog.Info("ParseProjectSessions: Completed parsing", "sessions", len(p.Sessions), "duration", formatDuration(totalDuration))
228		slog.Debug("ParseProjectSessions: Performance breakdown",
229			"scan", formatDuration(scanDuration),
230			"dedup", formatDuration(dedupDuration),
231			"dag", formatDuration(dagDuration),
232			"flatten", formatDuration(flattenDuration))
233		return nil
234	}
```

```text
540	func (p *JSONLParser) buildDAGs(records []JSONLRecord) [][]JSONLRecord {
541		// Create a map for quick lookup by uuid
542		recordByUuid := make(map[string]JSONLRecord)
543		for _, record := range records {
544			if uuid, ok := record.Data["uuid"].(string); ok {
545				recordByUuid[uuid] = record
546			}
547		}
548	
549		// Find all root nodes (parentUuid == null)
550		roots := []JSONLRecord{}
551		for _, record := range records {
552			if record.Data["parentUuid"] == nil {
553				roots = append(roots, record)
554			}
555		}
556	
557		// Build a DAG for each root
558		dags := [][]JSONLRecord{}
559		for _, root := range roots {
560			dag := p.buildDAGFromRoot(root, recordByUuid)
561			if len(dag) > 0 {
562				dags = append(dags, dag)
563			}
564		}
565	
566		return dags
567	}
568	
569	// buildDAGFromRoot builds a DAG starting from a root node
570	func (p *JSONLParser) buildDAGFromRoot(root JSONLRecord, recordByUuid map[string]JSONLRecord) []JSONLRecord {
571		dag := []JSONLRecord{}
572		visited := make(map[string]bool)
573	
574		var traverse func(node JSONLRecord)
575		traverse = func(node JSONLRecord) {
576			uuid, ok := node.Data["uuid"].(string)
577			if !ok || visited[uuid] {
578				return
579			}
580	
581			visited[uuid] = true
582			dag = append(dag, node)
583	
584			// Find all children and collect them first
585			children := []JSONLRecord{}
586			for _, record := range recordByUuid {
587				parentUuid, ok := record.Data["parentUuid"].(string)
588				if ok && parentUuid == uuid {
589					// Check if this child exists in our map (to handle orphans)
590					if _, exists := recordByUuid[record.Data["uuid"].(string)]; exists {
591						children = append(children, record)
592					}
593				}
594			}
595	
596			// Sort children by timestamp for deterministic traversal order
597			sort.Slice(children, func(i, j int) bool {
598				tsI, _ := children[i].Data["timestamp"].(string)
599				tsJ, _ := children[j].Data["timestamp"].(string)
600				return tsI < tsJ
601			})
602	
603			// Traverse children in sorted order
604			for _, child := range children {
605				traverse(child)
606			}
607		}
608	
609		traverse(root)
610		return dag
611	}
612
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 14:14:33Z)**_

I now have a complete picture. Let me give you the analysis.

## What commit e45daa3 actually fixed

That commit killed **two** quadratics in `reindex`, neither of which is what's slowing down `sync`:

1. **Codex by-id tree walk** — `buildSession` re-discovered each session by walking the whole `~/.codex/sessions` tree per session. Fixed with `spi.PathSessionReader` so Codex parses the already-known `NativePath`. Claude doesn't implement this and never had the problem.
2. **FTS5 delete full-scan** — every index write did `DELETE FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent=? AND session_id=?` on `UNINDEXED` columns, scanning the whole FTS table per row. Fixed by skipping the delete for brand-new rows (`IsNew`). This one *did* hit every provider — but only on `reindex`'s write path.

## Does `sync` have the same issue?

**No — it has a different, much bigger O(N²), in a code path the reindex fix never touched.** I profiled `sync claude` against your cwd project (91 files, 156 MB, ~25k records):

```
File scanning   ........  0.97 s
Deduplication   ........  0.03 s
DAG building    ........ 52.60 s   ← the whole cost
DAG merging     ........  0.0004 s
Flattening      ........  0.01 s
Total parse     ........ 53.6 s
```

The culprit is `buildDAGFromRoot` in `pkg/providers/claudecode/jsonl_parser.go:586`. To find a node's children it **iterates the entire 25k-record `recordByUuid` map at every single node**:

```go
for _, record := range recordByUuid {            // O(R) — for EVERY node
    parentUuid, ok := record.Data["parentUuid"].(string)
    if ok && parentUuid == uuid { ... }
}
```

So traversing ~R reachable nodes × scanning all R records ≈ 25,412² ≈ **645M iterations** → the 52 seconds. The FTS-delete fix and the Codex path fix are irrelevant to this — they're in `reindex`'s enumerate/write pipeline, not in the Claude JSONL parser.

## Why didn't the reindex fix help here?

Because **`sync` and `reindex` parse Claude sessions through entirely different entry points:**

- **`sync`** calls `provider.GetAgentChatSessions(cwd)` → `ParseProjectSessionsWithProgress`, which loads **every JSONL file in the project at once** into one ~25k-record set and builds DAGs across all of them together. That single giant record set is what makes `buildDAGFromRoot` quadratic.
- **`reindex`** calls `GetAgentChatSession(cwd, sessionID)` → `ParseSingleSession` (provider.go:352), parsing **one session at a time**. Each record set is tiny, so the same `buildDAGFromRoot` scan is cheap. That's why reindex is fast on Claude even though it runs the identical parser code.

So the reindex fix was correct and complete *for reindex's quadratics* — it just never exercised this parser path.

## Does it point to duplication that shouldn't be there?

Two observations, one minor and one conceptual:

- **Minor latent dup of the FTS issue:** `sync` writes the index via `LiveIndexer.Record` → `Upsert`, which **never sets `IsNew`**, so it always pays the FTS delete-scan that reindex now skips. Right now your DB is only 832 rows, so this is ~76k scans — negligible, *not* what you're feeling. But it's the same class of bug, latent, and will grow with the index. Worth a note, not the fix.
- **The real point:** this is the **same *class* of bug the reindex commit was fighting** — "re-scan the whole collection per item instead of consulting a precomputed index" — just in a different place. The reindex fix's lesson ("enumeration already recorded the path, don't re-discover it") applies verbatim here: the records are already in a map, don't re-scan all of them to find children.

## Recommended fix

Build a `parentUuid → []children` adjacency map **once** (O(N)), then look children up directly instead of scanning. This turns the whole `buildDAGs` from O(N²) into O(N log N) (the `log` is the existing per-node timestamp sort), and should drop that 52 s to well under a second.

It's a localized change to `buildDAGs`/`buildDAGFromRoot` — no new files, no new dependencies, identical output ordering (same timestamp sort, same `visited` guard).

Options:

1. **(Recommended) Precompute children adjacency in `buildDAGs`, pass it to `buildDAGFromRoot`.** Smallest change, fixes the dominant 52 s, preserves behavior exactly. I'd add a test asserting DAG structure is unchanged on a fixture.
2. Also fix the latent `LiveIndexer`/`Upsert` FTS-delete dup at the same time (give `Upsert` an `IsNew`-style fast path or have sync pass it). Cheap, future-proofs the index as it grows, but not urgent.
3. Do nothing / just document.

I'd do **1 now**, and **2 as a small follow-up** since it's the same lesson. Want me to implement the `buildDAGs` fix (option 1), or both 1 and 2?

```text
614	func (p *JSONLParser) flattenDAG(dag []JSONLRecord) []JSONLRecord {
615		// Sort by timestamp, with parent-child relationships as tiebreaker
616		sort.Slice(dag, func(i, j int) bool {
617			tsI, _ := dag[i].Data["timestamp"].(string)
618			tsJ, _ := dag[j].Data["timestamp"].(string)
619			if tsI != tsJ {
620				return tsI < tsJ
621			}
622			// If timestamps are equal, check parent-child relationship
623			uuidI, _ := dag[i].Data["uuid"].(string)
624			uuidJ, _ := dag[j].Data["uuid"].(string)
625			parentI, _ := dag[i].Data["parentUuid"].(string)
626			parentJ, _ := dag[j].Data["parentUuid"].(string)
627	
628			// If j is the parent of i, j should come first
629			if parentI == uuidJ {
630				return false
631			}
632			// If i is the parent of j, i should come first
633			if parentJ == uuidI {
634				return true
635			}
636			// Otherwise, sort by UUID for deterministic ordering
637			return uuidI < uuidJ
638		})
639	
640		return dag
641	}
642

655		for i := range p.Records {
```

```text
270	// Upsert inserts or replaces a single session row and its full-text row, atomically. A
271	// re-index of the same session (same agent + session_id) replaces both in lockstep.
272	func (s *Store) Upsert(sess Session) error {
273		return s.UpsertBatch([]Session{sess})
274	}
275	
276	// UpsertBatch writes many sessions in one transaction — the write path for `reindex`,
277	// which batches to avoid thousands of tiny WAL commits.
278	func (s *Store) UpsertBatch(sessions []Session) error {
279		if len(sessions) == 0 {
280			return nil
281		}
282		tx, err := s.db.Begin()
283		if err != nil {
284			return fmt.Errorf("begin upsert: %w", err)
285		}
286		committed := false
287		defer func() {
288			if !committed {
289				_ = tx.Rollback()
290			}
291		}()
292	
293		st, closeStmts, err := prepareUpsert(tx)
294		if err != nil {
295			return err
296		}
297		defer closeStmts()
298	
299		for _, sess := range sessions {
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 14:39:56Z)**_

Everything passes. Here's what I did.

## Summary

The slowness in `specstory sync` was **not** the same issue commit e45daa3 fixed for `reindex` — it was a separate, larger O(N²) in a code path that commit never touched. I fixed that, plus the one latent piece of the reindex bug that *did* leak into the sync write path.

### Fix 1 — the real culprit (`buildDAGs`)

`sync` parses the **whole project's** JSONL files into one ~25k-record set and builds DAGs across all of them. `buildDAGFromRoot` found each node's children by **scanning the entire 25k-record map at every node** → ~645M iterations.

`pkg/providers/claudecode/jsonl_parser.go`: precompute a `parentUuid → []children` adjacency map once (and sort each child list once), then traverse by direct lookup. Same output ordering, same `visited` guard.

- **DAG building: 52.6s → 20.5ms** (~2500×)
- **Total parse: 53.6s → 1.0s**; full `sync claude`: 57s → 4.85s
- Identical results: 162 DAGs → 92 sessions, before and after

`reindex` never hit this because it parses **one session at a time** (`GetAgentChatSession` → `ParseSingleSession`), so its record set per call is tiny.

### Fix 2 — the latent FTS dup from the reindex bug

`sync` writes the index via `LiveIndexer.Record` → `Upsert`, which **always** ran the whole-table FTS delete (`session_id`/`agent` are `UNINDEXED`) that reindex now skips via `IsNew`. Small today (834 rows) but the same class of bug, and it grows with the index.

- `store.go`: added `Exists(agent, sessionID)` — an O(log n) primary-key lookup. Since the `sessions` row and `sessions_fts` row are written together, a missing `sessions` row proves there's no FTS row to delete.
- `reindex.go` (`LiveIndexer.Record`): on a session's first write this process, check `Exists` once; if absent, set `IsNew` so `Upsert` skips the scan. Re-records (watch/run growth, re-syncs) keep the delete.
- Verified: two full syncs → **zero duplicate FTS rows**, `sessions` and `sessions_fts` counts match exactly (834 = 834).

### Answering your original questions

- **Same issue as reindex? No.** Reindex's two quadratics were a Codex whole-tree walk and the FTS delete-scan on reindex's writer. Sync's dominant cost was a completely different quadratic in the Claude DAG builder.
- **Why didn't the reindex fix help? Different entry point.** Sync uses `GetAgentChatSessions` (all files at once); reindex uses per-session parsing. The reindex fixes lived in its enumerate/write pipeline, orthogonal to the parser.
- **Duplication? Yes, of a kind.** It's the *same lesson* — "don't re-scan the whole collection per item; consult a precomputed index" — recurring in a new place (Fix 1), plus one genuine leak of the reindex FTS bug into sync's write path (Fix 2).

I haven't committed anything (per your git rules). Want me to add a regression test that asserts `buildDAGs` produces identical structure on a multi-session fixture?

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 14:42:10Z)**_

Good — this changes my answer. The codebase already has substantial `buildDAGs` coverage, so I want to correct my earlier suggestion.

## What already exists

`jsonl_parser_test.go` has four `buildDAGs` tests, all table-driven with `validate` closures — exactly the house style:

- `TestBuildDAGs_NormalChain` — asserts DAG count, record count, and **exact ordering** (`root → child-1 → child-2`)
- `TestBuildDAGs_OrphanedRecords` — orphan handling
- `TestBuildDAGs_WithIsMetaRoot` — root detection
- `TestBuildDAGs_LargeDataset` — 10k records (deep chain + 100-session fan-out), `verifyDAGIntegrity`, **and a perf assertion** (20s ceiling)

So the "identical structure on a multi-session fixture" test I proposed is **largely redundant** — `NormalChain` + `OrphanedRecords` already lock in structure and ordering, and they passed unchanged after my refactor. That's low importance; I'd skip it.

## The real gap

`TestBuildDAGs_LargeDataset` is supposed to be the perf guard, but it **would not have caught this regression, and won't catch its reintroduction**:

- **Dataset too small:** 10k records. The old O(N²) code at 10k is ~100M inner steps ≈ ~8s. The blow-up only became painful at your real ~25k records (645M steps → 52s).
- **Ceiling too loose:** 20s. ~8s sails under it.

In other words, the quadratic was *already* exercised by an existing perf test and slipped through. That's the regression worth guarding against: someone "simplifies" `buildDAGFromRoot` back into a per-node scan, and nothing fails.

## What I'd actually do

Tighten the existing test rather than add a new one — same file, same pattern, no outlier:

- Bump the dataset to ~25–30k records (matches real projects like your cwd).
- Tighten the ceiling to ~2–3s. The fixed code does 25k in ~20ms, so 2–3s is a ~100× safety margin against flaky CI while still being ~20–50× below where O(N²) lands (~50s+).

**What regression it catches:** reintroduction of any super-linear children/parent lookup in DAG construction — the exact class of bug just fixed. It catches *performance*, not correctness (correctness is already covered).

**Is it the kind of test we have?** Yes — it's an edit to an existing table-driven perf test that's already structured this way. A standalone "golden structure" test would be the outlier (and redundant).

Net: importance is **moderate** — not because structure needs more coverage, but because the existing perf tripwire is mis-calibrated and let a 50-second regression through. Want me to tighten `TestBuildDAGs_LargeDataset`?

Done. The tripwire is calibrated and verified.

## What changed

Edited the existing `TestBuildDAGs_LargeDataset` in `jsonl_parser_test.go` — same table-driven shape, no new test:

- Both cases bumped **10k → 30k records** (single deep chain + 100-session fan-out). Dataset size is what makes the tripwire work — a quadratic only trips the ceiling once N is large enough.
- Ceiling tightened **20s → 3s**, with a comment explaining it guards against reintroducing the O(N²) children lookup.

## Verification

- Fixed (linear) builder: **18ms** (single chain) / **11.7ms** (fan-out) — a ~150–250× margin under the 3s ceiling, so it won't flake on slow CI.
- The old O(N²) code at 30k would be ~900M inner steps ≈ 70s+ — comfortably over 3s, so the tripwire genuinely catches the regression class (whereas at the old 10k/20s settings it didn't).
- Full `claudecode` package passes; `golangci-lint` clean.

Nothing committed, per your git rules. All three changes are now in place: the `buildDAGs` fix, the `LiveIndexer`/`Exists` FTS fix, and this perf guard.

```text
348	func (s *Store) UnattributedCount(unknownID string) (int, error) {

354	// ListByProject returns a project's sessions, newest activity first. Body is not
355	// populated (it lives only in the FTS index). Used by the `specstory resume` picker.
356	func (s *Store) ListByProject(projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
357		rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT `+sessionColumns+`
358			FROM sessions WHERE project_id = ? ORDER BY updated_at DESC, created_at DESC`, projectID)
359		if err != nil {
360			return nil, err
361		}
362		defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
363		return scanSessions(rows)
364	}
365	
366	// ProjectSummary is a rolled-up view of one project for the all-projects picker.
367	type ProjectSummary struct {
368		ProjectID    string
369		ProjectName  string
370		Sessions     int            // total sessions in the project
371		LastActivity string         // most recent updated_at across the project
372		AgentCounts  map[string]int // sessions per agent (claude, codex, …)
373	}
374	
375	// ListProjects returns one rolled-up summary per project, most recently active first.
376	// Used by the all-projects view (date-bucketed). The unknown-project bucket is included;
377	// the caller decides how to present it.
378	func (s *Store) ListProjects() ([]ProjectSummary, error) {
379		rows, err := s.db.Query(`SELECT project_id, project_name, agent, COUNT(*), MAX(updated_at)
380			FROM sessions GROUP BY project_id, agent`)
381		if err != nil {
382			return nil, err
383		}
384		defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
385	
386		byID := map[string]*ProjectSummary{}
387		for rows.Next() {
388			var pid, pname, agent, last string
389			var n int
390			if err := rows.Scan(&pid, &pname, &agent, &n, &last); err != nil {
391				return nil, err
392			}
393			ps, ok := byID[pid]
394			if !ok {
395				ps = &ProjectSummary{ProjectID: pid, ProjectName: pname, AgentCounts: map[string]int{}}
396				byID[pid] = ps
397			}
398			ps.Sessions += n
399			ps.AgentCounts[agent] += n
400			if ps.ProjectName == "" {
401				ps.ProjectName = pname
402			}
403			if last > ps.LastActivity {
404				ps.LastActivity = last
405			}
406		}
407		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
408			return nil, err
409		}
410	
411		out := make([]ProjectSummary, 0, len(byID))
412		for _, ps := range byID {
413			out = append(out, *ps)
414		}
415		sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i].LastActivity > out[j].LastActivity })
416		return out, nil
417	}
418	
419	// SessionBody returns the full-text conversation body for a session (for the preview
420	// pane), or "" if the session has no indexed body (e.g. Cursor, metadata-only).
421	func (s *Store) SessionBody(agent, sessionID string) (string, error) {
422		var body string
423		err := s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT body FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent = ? AND session_id = ?`,
424			agent, sessionID).Scan(&body)
425		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
426			return "", nil
427		}
428		return body, err

430	
431	// Search runs a full-text query and returns matching sessions, most recent first.
432	// projectID scopes the search to one project; "" searches across all projects.
433	func (s *Store) Search(query, projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
434		return s.SearchContext(context.Background(), query, projectID)
435	}
436	
437	// SearchContext is Search bound to a context. The interactive search cancels the context
438	// when a newer keystroke arrives, which aborts a slow in-flight query and frees its
439	// connection. Snippets are deliberately fetched separately for only the visible rows:
440	// FTS5 snippet generation over hundreds of full transcripts dominates broad searches.
441	func (s *Store) SearchContext(ctx context.Context, query, projectID string) ([]Session, error) {
442		q := `SELECT ` + prefixed("s", sessionColumns) + `
443			FROM sessions_fts
444			JOIN sessions s ON s.agent = sessions_fts.agent AND s.session_id = sessions_fts.session_id
445			WHERE sessions_fts MATCH ?`
446		args := []any{query}
447		if projectID != "" {
448			q += ` AND s.project_id = ?`
449			args = append(args, projectID)
450		}
451		// Order by recency, not FTS5's BM25 rank. For full-conversation transcripts BM25 is
452		// noisy (length-normalization dominates) and a single-term query gets no IDF signal,
453		// so relevance order looks arbitrary; newest-first is what's useful when browsing your
454		// own history. It also makes LIMIT keep the 500 most RECENT matches (vs the 500 densest)
455		// and skips BM25 scoring entirely. updated_at is ISO 8601, so TEXT sort = chronological.
456		q += ` ORDER BY s.updated_at DESC LIMIT 500`
457	
458		rows, err := s.db.QueryContext(ctx, q, args...)
459		if err != nil {
460			return nil, err
461		}
462		defer func() { _ = rows.Close() }()
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 14:52:00Z)**_

Here's the grounded answer from the actual query plans.

## The regular `sessions` table is well-covered

Every `WHERE`/`JOIN` filter already hits an index — nothing missing there:

| Query | Plan | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `Exists`, Search JOIN | covering index on PK `(agent, session_id)` | ✅ |
| `ProjectCount`, `UnattributedCount`, `CountByProject` | covering `idx_sessions_project` | ✅ |
| `ListByProject` (resume picker) | `idx_sessions_project` filter **+ TEMP B-TREE for ORDER BY** | sort step |
| `ListProjects` | full index scan **+ TEMP B-TREE for GROUP BY** | inherent |

The only plain-table candidate is a composite to absorb `ListByProject`'s sort:

```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_project_recent ON sessions(project_id, updated_at DESC, created_at DESC);
```

But the payoff is **marginal**: it sorts *one project's* sessions (hundreds at most), once, on a user keypress. `ListProjects` scans the whole table regardless (it's a full-corpus aggregate), so its temp-b-tree is inherent. I would **not** add either speculatively — they're real sorts but on bounded/unavoidable workloads. Add the composite only if the picker ever feels slow on a giant project.

## The real gap isn't index-able — it's the FTS5 UNINDEXED lookups

Two queries do a **full `SCAN sessions_fts`** because `session_id`/`agent` are `UNINDEXED`, and you *cannot* `CREATE INDEX` on FTS5 UNINDEXED columns:

- **`SessionBody`** → `SELECT body FROM sessions_fts WHERE agent=? AND session_id=?` — **unmitigated, hot-ish read.** It's the TUI preview-pane fallback (`session_tui.go:1140`), firing once per session you select. At 832 rows it's nothing; at 28k rows every preview render scans the whole FTS content table.
- **The FTS delete** → same full scan, but already mitigated by the `IsNew` skip we just added.

This is the *same class* as the bug we just fixed — a point operation forced into a full scan because there's no key index — and `SessionBody` is the read-side analog that's still unguarded.

The proper fix is a **rowid mapping**, not an index: add an `fts_rowid` column to `sessions`, capture `last_insert_rowid()` right after the FTS insert, then both the body read and the delete become `WHERE rowid = ?` (O(1)). That bonus: the delete becomes unconditionally O(1), so the `IsNew` special-case could retire entirely.

## Recommendation

- **No plain-table index is meaningfully missing.** The `ListByProject` composite is optional; skip it absent evidence.
- The thing actually worth doing is the **FTS-rowid mapping**, which fixes the unmitigated `SessionBody` scan and simplifies the delete path. But it's a schema + write-path change (needs a `reindexVersion` bump and `docs/SESSIONS-DB.md` update), so it's a separate decision, not a one-line index.

Given your DB is currently 832 rows, none of this is urgent. Want me to implement the FTS-rowid mapping now (so it's correct before the index grows), or leave it documented as a known scaling item?

---

_**User (2026-06-29 14:53:57Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/SESSIONS-DB.md`

```text
405	| `created_at`    | TEXT    | ISO 8601 session creation timestamp (first turn). Powers the "Created" sort.                                                                           |
406	| `updated_at`    | TEXT    | ISO 8601 last-activity timestamp (last turn, else file mtime). Powers "X ago", the "Updated" sort, and resume-last.                                    |
407	| `user_turns`    | INTEGER | Count of user prompts — the headline "how much work is here" signal.                                                                                   |
408	| `total_turns`   | INTEGER | Count of all messages (user + agent). Computed alongside `user_turns`.                                                                                 |
409	| `slug`          | TEXT    | Filename-safe slug derived from the first user message.                                                                                                |
410	| `name`          | TEXT    | Human-readable session description (may be empty).                                                                                                     |
411	| `native_path`   | TEXT    | Absolute path the provider opens to read this session — a JSONL/JSON file for most agents, or a per-session `store.db` for Cursor. Unique per session. |
412	| `origin_cwd`    | TEXT    | Working directory the session was launched from (the input to identity resolution).                                                                    |
413	| `size`          | INTEGER | Native file size in bytes — part of the freshness fingerprint (incremental-skip on re-run).                                                            |
414	| `mtime`         | INTEGER | Native file modification time, epoch ms — part of the freshness fingerprint.                                                                           |
415	| `index_version` | INTEGER | reindex logic version that wrote the row — part of the fingerprint; bumping it forces a full re-parse.                                                 |
416	| `indexed_at`    | TEXT    | ISO 8601 time this row was last written by `reindex`.                                                                                                  |
417	
418	Primary key: `(agent, session_id)` — a session is unique within a provider, and belongs to
419	exactly one project. `project_id` is indexed for per-project filtering. `created_at` /
420	`updated_at` / `user_turns` / `total_turns` come from the full `SessionData` parse `reindex`
421	already performs; the rest come from the lightweight enumeration ref.
422	
423	**Considered and deliberately excluded (first cut):**
424	
425	- **Git branch.** Claude Code records `gitBranch` per turn (stable within a session), but Codex
426	  and the others record nothing — a half-populated, Claude-only column isn't worth it yet. If
427	  added later it belongs on the enumeration ref as native metadata, *not* in neutral
428	  `SessionData`.
429	- **User naming / rename.** No `custom_name`; sessions are not renameable in the first cut.
430	  Keeps `sessions` a purely rebuildable cache (no user-authoritative fields to preserve across
431	  `reindex`).
432	- **Lineage.** No `source_session_id`. The breadcrumb already exists natively for free —
433	  reconstruction writes `specstorySourceSessionId` (the source session id) into the rebuilt
434	  file's meta (`claudecode`/`codexcli`/`droidcli` `reconstruct.go`) — so it can be surfaced
435	  later (ideally also capturing the source *agent*, which that field does not) when fork/resume
436	  lineage UX is actually designed.
437	
438	### `sessions_fts` — FTS5 full-text index (kept in tandem with `sessions`)
439	
440	| Column       | In FTS index?    | Description                                                                                     |
441	|--------------|------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
442	| `session_id` | No (`UNINDEXED`) | Join key back to `sessions`; stored in the row but not tokenized.                               |
443	| `agent`      | No (`UNINDEXED`) | Join key back to `sessions`; stored in the row but not tokenized.                               |
444	| `name`       | Yes              | Session description / first-message-derived name; tokenized + searchable.                       |
445	| `body`       | Yes              | Full conversation text — the reconstruction-flattened user/agent turns; tokenized + searchable. |
446	
447	`UNINDEXED` is an FTS5 per-column keyword meaning the column is **stored but excluded from the
448	full-text index** — not "lacks a b-tree index" (FTS5 tables have no secondary b-tree indexes at
449	all). The two join keys ride along in the row so a `MATCH` hit maps straight back to its
450	`sessions` row without a separate lookup table. Default FTS5 tokenizer. Rows are
451	inserted/replaced alongside their `sessions` row during
452	`reindex`; deletion of a `sessions` row removes its `sessions_fts` row.
453	
454	## Non-Goals (first cut)
455	
456	- **The picker rewiring.** `specstory resume`'s interactive flow still uses the project-scoped
457	  `ListAgentChatSessions` until a later chunk points it at `sessions.db`.
458	- **Storing serialized `SessionData`.** The first cut stores metadata + full-text body, not a
459	  `SessionData` blob. A blob becomes relevant for cloud Stages 3–4 (where the cloud is the
460	  source). Deferred.
461	
462	## Deferred — DO NEXT (Cursor cwd recovery)
463	
464	Cursor CLI sessions are currently enumerated with `OriginCwd=""` and therefore indexed
```

```text
99		s := openTemp(t)
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-29 15:00:38Z)**_

The FTS-rowid mapping is implemented, verified, and tested. Here's the summary.

## What changed

**`pkg/sessionindex/store.go`** — the mapping itself:
- **Schema:** added `fts_rowid INTEGER` to the `sessions` table + an idempotent `ALTER TABLE` migration. New `sessionInsertColumns` const (= `sessionColumns` + `fts_rowid`); reads still use `sessionColumns`, so `fts_rowid` stays write-only and out of the `Session` struct.
- **Write path (`upsertOne`):** now inserts the FTS row **first**, captures its `last_insert_rowid()`, and stores it on the `sessions` row. For existing sessions, `deleteOldFTSRow` resolves the prior FTS row by its stored rowid (O(1) `DELETE ... WHERE rowid = ?`) instead of the old whole-FTS scan. New sessions (`IsNew`) skip the lookup+delete entirely. `prepareUpsert` was refactored to a slice-based statement closer (cleaner error handling for the now-5 statements).
- **Read path (`SessionBody`):** joins `sessions_fts ON rowid = sessions.fts_rowid` — O(1) — with a one-time by-key fallback for NULL `fts_rowid` rows.

**`pkg/cmd/reindex.go`** — bumped `reindexVersion` 5 → 6 so existing rows re-parse once and populate `fts_rowid`.

**`docs/SESSIONS-DB.md`** — documented the new column and *why* FTS rows are reached by rowid, never by the UNINDEXED keys.

**`pkg/sessionindex/store_test.go`** — added `TestUpsertHandlesLegacyNullRowid` covering the migration-window fallback (NULL `fts_rowid`): body read falls back, re-index deletes the stale row by key without duplicating, and `fts_rowid` repopulates.

## Verified against the live DB (after reindex)

- Query plan flipped from `SCAN sessions_fts VIRTUAL TABLE` (full scan) → PK search + `VIRTUAL TABLE INDEX 0:=` (rowid equality).
- 832/838 rows populated `fts_rowid` (the 6 NULLs are live-indexed sessions outside enumeration — the fallback's exact purpose); **0 orphaned** rowids, **0 duplicate** FTS rows, `sessions` = `sessions_fts` = 838.
- Body identical across the rowid fast path and the by-key fallback.
- Full suite (17 packages) green; lint 0 issues.

Both the unmitigated `SessionBody` read scan and the per-row delete are now O(1) regardless of index size. Nothing committed, per your git rules.

This is a good moment to capture what we found, since it spans three commits' worth of context. Want me to save a short project memory noting that `sync`'s slowness was a distinct `buildDAGs` O(N²) (not the reindex FTS/Codex fixes) and that the FTS path is now rowid-keyed? It'd save re-deriving this next time someone profiles the indexer.
